StudentApplication.title: StudentApplication
StudentApplication.description: Represents a student application submitted to the CampusConnect ERP system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the student application.

dateOfBirth.description: Date of birth of the applicant.

program.description: The program the applicant is applying for.

applicationDate.description: Date when the application was submitted.

adminId.description: Reference to Admin who reviewed the application. (Relationship: Admin 1:N StudentApplication)

TeacherApplication.title: TeacherApplication
TeacherApplication.description: Represents a teacher application submitted to the CampusConnect ERP system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the teacher application.

dateOfBirth.description: Date of birth of the applicant.

applicationDate.description: Date when the application was submitted.

adminId.description: Reference to Admin who reviewed the application. (Relationship: Admin 1:N TeacherApplication)

Admin.title: Admin
Admin.description: Represents an administrator user in the CampusConnect ERP system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the admin user.

definition.description: Stores student application data. Requires 'adminId' field referencing the admin who reviewed the application for authorization.

params.name: studentApplicationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the student application.

definition.description: Stores teacher application data. Requires 'adminId' field referencing the admin who reviewed the application for authorization.

params.name: teacherApplicationId
params.description: Unique identifier for the teacher application.

definition.description: Stores admin role. Existence of document implies admin role for the user.

params.name: adminId
params.description: Unique identifier for the admin user.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to manage student and teacher applications within the CampusConnect ERP system, ensuring secure access and efficient processing. It segregates applications into separate collections for student and teacher applicants, allowing for distinct security rules and data management. The design prioritizes Authorization Independence by ensuring that access control does not rely on hierarchical `get()` calls. Instead, it uses role-based access control via the `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection, and explicit `adminId` fields within application documents to link applications to reviewing admins. 

The structure supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters) by separating application types into distinct collections (student_applications, teacher_applications) which allows listing operations with specific criteria such as `status` to be performed efficiently without compromising security.
